channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Curated geopolitical news headlines from global sources

item.title: Man Kills 8 Children in Louisiana Mass Shooting, Deadliest US Attack in Two Years
item.description: A gunman killed eight children aged 1 to 14 in domestic-related shootings at two homes in Louisiana before being shot dead by police. It was the deadliest mass shooting in the United States in more than two years, igniting debates over gun violence.

item.title: Bulgaria's Pro-Russia Former President Radev Wins Landslide Election Victory
item.description: Progress Bulgaria, the Kremlin-friendly coalition led by former President Rumen Radev, is on track to win 37-38% of the vote according to exit polls. The result could end years of weak coalition governments and shift the EU member's foreign policy orientation toward Moscow.

item.title: Ukraine Carries Out First-Ever Shahed Interception Using Naval Drone Platform
item.description: Ukraine's military carried out the first-ever interception of a Russian Shahed drone using a naval drone platform operated by the 412th Nemesis Brigade. The breakthrough demonstrates Kyiv's growing innovation in maritime defense capabilities against Russian aerial threats.

item.title: Ukraine Strikes Russian Oil Refinery in Krasnodar Krai, Major Fire Reported
item.description: Ukrainian drones struck the Tuapse Oil Refinery in southern Russia's Krasnodar Krai during an overnight attack, causing a major fire. Kyiv continues its campaign to disrupt Moscow's war funding by targeting Russian energy infrastructure.

item.title: Memorial Day 2026: 174 IDF Soldiers and 79 Israeli Civilians Killed in Past Year
item.description: Israel's Memorial Day 2026 commemorates 174 IDF soldiers and 79 Israeli civilians killed over the past year, a significant increase reflecting the ongoing multi-front war. The toll underscores the human cost of the Iran war and Gaza conflict.

item.title: UK Police Investigate Iran Links to Arson Attacks on Jewish Sites in London
item.description: British police are investigating possible Iranian links to a series of arson attacks on Jewish targets in London, with the UK chief rabbi warning of a 'sustained campaign of violence' against the Jewish community gathering momentum.

item.title: US Military Strike Kills Three on Alleged Drug Boat in Caribbean
item.description: The US military killed three people in a strike on a boat accused of ferrying drugs in the Caribbean Sea, continuing the Trump administration's campaign against 'narcoterrorism' that has now killed at least 181 people.

item.title: Canada's Strong US Economic Ties Are Now a Weakness, PM Carney Says
item.description: Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney said the country's strong economic ties to the United States were once a strength but are now a weakness that must be corrected, citing Trump's tariffs and growing uncertainty.

item.title: Restoring Oil Flows Through Hormuz Will Take Months or Years Even After War Ends
item.description: Even if the guns fall silent, oil and gas flows through the Strait of Hormuz will take months—possibly years—to recover to pre-war levels. The stop-start shipping traffic underscores profound uncertainty hanging over the world's most critical energy chokepoint.

item.title: US Navy Destroyer Disables Iranian Cargo Ship Breaking Blockade
item.description: The USS Spruance fired on the Iranian-flagged M/V Touska after a six-hour standoff when the vessel attempted to reach Bandar Abbas in violation of the US naval blockade. Marines boarded the ship, which remains in US custody, while Iran vowed retaliation calling it 'armed piracy.'

item.title: Ukraine Unveils New Koral Surface-to-Air Missile for Domestic Air Defense
item.description: Ukraine displayed what appears to be the Koral medium-range surface-to-air missile during a defense industry exhibition, with an estimated range of 100km and anti-ballistic missile capability. The domestically developed SAM could help offset Ukraine's shortage of Western air defense systems.

item.title: Pakistani Journalist Says Iran's Military Strength Delivered Strategic Results
item.description: A senior Pakistani journalist argues Iran achieved strategic gains in its war with the US and Israel, including establishing control over the Strait of Hormuz and potentially accelerating the reduction of the American military footprint in the Middle East.

item.title: Iran Will Never Cede Control of Strait of Hormuz, Senior Politician Tells BBC
item.description: Iranian lawmaker Ebrahim Azizi told the BBC that Tehran will 'never' give up control of the Strait of Hormuz, calling it an 'inalienable right.' He said Iran is introducing a bill to enshrine its control over the strategic waterway in law.

item.title: Zelensky Condemns US Extension of Russian Sanctions Waiver Amid Energy Crisis
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky condemned the US decision to extend a waiver allowing Russia to sell oil already loaded on vessels until May 16. Zelensky said 'every dollar paid for Russian oil is money for the war.'

item.title: US Businesses Can Begin Claiming Tariff Refunds Monday After Supreme Court Ruling
item.description: A refund system for businesses that paid tariffs the Supreme Court ruled unconstitutional is set to launch Monday. Over 330,000 importers paid about $166 billion on 53 million shipments, with 56,497 importers eligible for $127 billion in refunds including interest.

item.title: Asia's Energy Pain Far From Over as LNG Shock Hits World's Factory
item.description: Asian economies are grappling with tightened LNG supply, mounting foreign exchange pressure and damage already locked into Q2 performance as the Iran war disrupts 20% of global oil and LNG flows. The Philippines' GDP growth was downgraded to 3.7% from projected 5-6%.

item.title: India Faces Fertiliser Crisis as Iran War Disrupts Gulf Supplies
item.description: India's urea imports have surged to $935 per tonne from $508 pre-war, with ammonia facilities in Qatar and Saudi Arabia shut after Iranian strikes. Domestic urea production has fallen from 2.5 million tonnes monthly to just 1.5 million tonnes due to LNG supply disruptions.

item.title: Australia's Big Banks Brace for Oil Shock Fallout as Hormuz Remains Blocked
item.description: Australia's major lenders Westpac and National Australia Bank are increasing bad loan provisions as the prolonged Hormuz closure begins impacting the economy. The government has announced a $1 billion emergency loan program to help businesses weather the fuel crisis.

item.title: North Korea Tests Cluster Warhead Missiles With Kim Jong Un Observing
item.description: North Korean leader Kim Jong Un supervised test-launches of the upgraded Hwasongpho-11 Ra tactical ballistic missile with cluster bomb and fragmentation mine warheads. Five missiles struck a 12.5-13 hectare target area with 'very high density.'

item.title: Spain Urges EU to End Association Agreement With Israel Over International Law Violations
item.description: Spain announced it will ask the European Union to end its association agreement with Israel over alleged violations of international law. The move marks a significant escalation in European diplomatic pressure on Israel.

item.title: Japan's Taiwan Strait Warship Transit on Treaty Anniversary Raises New Flashpoint With China
item.description: A Japanese warship's transit of the Taiwan Strait on the anniversary of a key security treaty drew sharp criticism from Beijing, which said the passage 'harmed the feelings of the Chinese people.' Tensions in East Asia reached a fresh peak.

item.title: Japan Self-Defense Forces to Join Philippine-US Military Drills in South China Sea
item.description: Japan's Self-Defense Forces will take part in annual military drills led by the Philippines and the US, testing Tokyo's commitment to security in the South China Sea. The exercises come as Japan expands its regional defense partnerships.

item.title: Bahrain Reviews Citizenship Laws After Foiling Iran-Linked Cells
item.description: Bahrain's King Hamad announced measures against those who 'betrayed' the country after authorities foiled Iran-linked cells. The Gulf state is reviewing citizenship laws, potentially stripping nationality from dissidents.

item.title: Two US Embassy Officials Die in Car Crash in Mexico After Drug Operation
item.description: Two US embassy officials died in a car crash in Chihuahua alongside two Mexican agents following a drug operation. The officials were trainers working with Mexican counterparts as part of ongoing bilateral security cooperation efforts.

item.title: US Navy Seizes Iranian Cargo Ship Near Strait of Hormuz, Tehran Vows Response
item.description: The US Navy fired on and seized an Iranian-flagged cargo ship near the Strait of Hormuz after a six-hour standoff, with Iran's military calling it 'piracy' and vowing a swift response. The incident throws the fragile ceasefire into question.
